title: In cxxrtl case item expressions are compared in an unexpected order
body: ### Version Yosys 0.33+65 (git sha1 934c82254, clang 14.0.0-1ubuntu1.1 -fPIC -Os) ### On which OS did this happen? Linux ### Reproduction Steps Verilog file `top.v`: ```Verilog module top ( input clk, input [1:0] select, output reg [1:0] result ); always @(posedge clk) begin case (select) 2'b00: result <= 2'b11; 2'b01: result <= 2'b10; 2'b10: result <= 2'b01; default: result <= 2'b00; endcase end endmodule ``` Command: ``` yosys -p "read_verilog -formal top.v; hierarchy -check -top top; write_cxxrtl top.cpp" ``` ### Expected Behavior According to section 9.5 of Verilog Standard: > The case expression given in parentheses shall be evaluated exactly once and before any of the case item expressions. The case item expressions shall be evaluated and compared **in the exact order in which they are given**. If there is a default case item, it is ignored during this linear search. During the linear search, if one of the case item expressions matches the case expression given in parentheses, then the statement associated with that case item shall be executed, and the linear search shall terminate. ### Actual Behavior By checking `p_top::eval()` in `top.cpp`, I found it first evaluates the third item in the case expression. ```cpp p_result.next = (eq_uu<1>(p_select, value<2>{0x2u}) ? value<2>{0x1u} : (eq_uu<1>(p_select, value<2>{0x1u}) ? value<2>{0x2u} : (logic_not<1>(p_select) ? value<2>{0x3u} : value<2>{0u}))); ``` --- In the situation above, the comparison order won't lead to incorrect results in the simulation. However, the order does matter when there are overlaps between case items. Here is another example I found in Verilog Standard: Therefore, it may be necessary to address this issue.
code_blocks: yosys -p "read_verilog -formal top.v; hierarchy -check -top top; write_cxxrtl top.cpp"
